description_long: This traffic barricade is a classic A-frame sawhorse-style barrier, commonly used for roadwork and pedestrian control in urban environments. It features two wide, rectangular panels with alternating orange and white diagonal stripes, designed for high visibility and to signal caution. The frame is constructed from what appears to be metal, with four sturdy legs providing stability and a crossbar for additional support. The striped panels are attached to the front and back, while the sides are open, showing the structural framework. The top of the barricade has two circular hinge points, suggesting it can be folded for transport or storage. The overall finish is slightly weathered, indicating outdoor use, and there are no visible lights or warning signage beyond the reflective striping.
description: A metal A-frame traffic barricade with orange and white diagonal reflective stripes and a sturdy, foldable design. It features two striped panels and four legs for stability.
description_view_0: Front view showing both orange and white striped panels and the full width of the barricade.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile, open frame, and hinge points at the top.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped panels and crossbar support.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and structural frame.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal orange and white stripes, alternating, medium width, repeated across both panels.
